topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a dull, heavy stone. I stared at the screen, my jaw tightening until my molars ached. The headline was mine, the piece I’d poured weeks of late nights into, but the byline belonged to a stranger. A cold knot formed in my stomach, spreading outward like ink in water. I reread the attribution, the unfamiliar name mocking me. A slow, bitter laugh bubbled up, catching in my throat before escaping as a dry, rasping sound. I leaned back in my chair, the worn fabric groaning under my weight, and rubbed my temples, trying to physically scrub the confusion away. Every word felt tainted, every insight I’d offered now belonging to someone else.

[story 2]

I picked up the printed copy of the article. The paper felt flimsy in my hands, a betrayal of the effort I’d invested. My gaze swept over the text, lingering on the signature that wasn't mine. A deep sigh escaped me, a sound heavy with disappointment. I crumpled the paper slightly, the crinkle echoing the tightness in my chest. Why? Who had taken my voice and polished it into something else? I paced the small apartment, the floorboards protesting beneath my bare feet. My hands clenched into fists, the tension radiating up my arms. The silence of the room amplified the hollow feeling in my gut, a profound sense of being overlooked and disrespected.

[story 3]

When the notification popped up, I didn't move. I just sat there, staring blankly at the glowing text. My shoulders slumped forward, a heavy, defeated posture. The realization hit not with a sudden shock, but with a slow, creeping dread. I slowly reached for my coffee mug, the ceramic warm against my chilled fingers, but I didn't drink. Instead, I turned the monitor off, plunging the room into shadow. The air felt thick, suffocating. I let out a long, drawn-out breath, the sound weak and utterly devoid of energy. It was just… pointless. A quiet, simmering resentment began to build beneath the surface, a low thrumming that vibrated through my bones.
